risk_explanation: Risk Score: 68.5/100 ΓÇö High risk. Contributing factors: high blood pressure, elevated glucose levels, active smoking habit, low physical activity, family medical history. Immediate medical consultation is recommended.
recommendations: Monitor caloric intake and maintain active daily routines to reach an optimal BMI.
Schedule a consultation with a cardiologist to monitor and manage blood pressure.
Reduce sodium intake and avoid high-stress environments.
Limit refined sugars and simple carbohydrates to stabilize blood glucose levels.
Consider consulting an endocrinologist if glucose levels remain elevated.
Strongly advise engaging in a smoking cessation program immediately to drastically lower risks.
Gradually introduce physical activity, aiming for 150 minutes of moderate exercise spread across the week.
